claim: Prediction in atom ATOM_T010_S008_PRE_01 ("Okay, so what's gonna happen in this little model is if it wants to produce a...") holds if and only if the first-principle constraint in atom ATOM_T013_S025_FIR_01 ("But we are not losing the sight of the fact that it is in principle possible ...") is binding.
first_principles_validity_hypothesis: The prediction "Okay, so what's gonna happen in this little model is if i..." is forced by the underlying constraint "But we are not losing the sight of the fact that it is in..."; if the constraint is not binding the prediction may still hold by accident but the binding case is the load-bearing version of this candidate.
why_novel_vs_speaker: The speaker introduces the prediction (snippet S008) and the first-principle (snippet S025) in separate contexts; the speaker does not state that the second forces the first.
why_potentially_useful: A prediction that is forced by a first-principle is more falsifiable than a free-floating prediction: if the principle holds and the prediction fails, one of them is wrong, which is a binary research question.
